issues.description: WIN-053.c5 H3: Sub-terrestrial return-channel defender rescue not preempted. A dome defender can argue the south-pole coefficient describes the re-entry aperture above the Sump (symmetric with north). This rescue fails because: (a) symmetric circuit requires A=C, but fitted values differ by 4%; (b) both match observed WMM pole strengths which are asymmetric due to Earth's geodynamo, not dome geometry. Add ~3-sentence preemption paragraph. Curmudgeon recommendation: "A dome defender might argue the 64,852 nT coefficient describes the surface re-entry aperture above the Sump (symmetric with the north aperture), not the return-channel field itself. But that rescue requires A = C by construction (two identical apertures in a closed symmetric circuit), while the fitted values differ by 4% AND match the observationally-asymmetric WMM2025 pole strengths — which are asymmetric because Earth's real geodynamo is asymmetric, not because of any dome-predicted mechanism. The symmetric-aperture rescue is incompatible with the fitted coefficients."

issues.title: WIN-014: PRED-TIER-3 10–13 µGal target derivation unspecified — dome-based ΔB station not named
issues.description: WIN-014.c7 H1 [major]: detail_evidence and section 6.14 assert that PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal target derives from "ΔB/κ at named stations" without specifying which dome-based ΔB value (κ is fixed, but which ΔB?). A defender says the vise arithmetic is non-obvious without knowing the assumed ΔB. Make explicit: if we assume ΔB = 10–13 nT from the 1999 Membach/Strasbourg SG sites, then 10/1.677 ≈ 6.0 µGal and 13/1.677 ≈ 7.8 µGal — these are BELOW 10 µGal, not 10–13. The 10–13 µGal range in detail_evidence needs to be derived explicitly and shown to be achievable from the dome's own parameters.
